chapters.title: Your outfit's power
chapters.text:
Everyone has a favorite color, and the type of color a person likes can say a lot about their personality. For instance, black is associated with mystery and death, so people are often perceived as sad if they like or wear this color. Red is linked with sexuality, romance, war, danger, warning, blood, and flashing sirens. It captivates and stirs us into action. While some cultures see a love for red as a mark of an outgoing personality, in other places, it's a color that screams caution and threat.

Cultural and historical contexts can also affect how we perceive colors; many color-related expressions, like feeling blue, experiencing blackouts, or being 'green' with envy, have centuries-old origins. The tradition of wearing white at weddings, which started after Queen Victoria wore it at her wedding, is a classic example. Before then, people used any color for their weddings.

The color itself is inherently neutral; it gains meaning only through our associations. That's why the same hue can symbolize different things in diverse cultures. For instance, prisoners wear orange in America, while monks wear it in Southeast Asia.

When you pick out colors for your outfits, consider choosing vibrant hues if you want to make a statement and attract attention. Red is a good example.

This color is hot and seductive. If you notice, men approach women who wear red to bars or parties more than those who wear other colors. And this isn't just a theory; research published in the European Journal of Psychology has proven it.

When selecting red for professional outfits, consider a subtle approach.

Again, the color you choose has to fit the context it's meant for. Wearing a red bodycon dress to an interview, for instance, can cause you to lose the opportunity. While men often find women in red appealing, women may view other women dressed in red as threatening and overly flirtatious.

The color orange’s bright hue is associated with freshness, fun, humor, and sunshine. But orange also has some unholy connotations; it's often associated with plastic waste and prison uniforms. Orange, however, makes us feel alert and calm.

Consider yellow; it has an aura of cheerfulness, happiness, and vitality. However, yellow does not appear suitable when worn head to toe. Green is a natural color signifying fertility, hope, new birth, and regeneration, and it also connotes good luck and freshness.

chapters.title: The link between attire and personality
chapters.text:
Often, you may feel disconnected from how you appear or have anxiety about what to wear, and this is not abnormal. Your clothes and accessories talk ; they say a lot about you and your mood. For instance, wearing heels to a corporate event will make you feel like an authority. Showing up to the same event dressed in sweats could portray you as one of those people who don't take themselves seriously, and you'll begin to feel that way. In other words, what you wear influences how you feel about situations — your mood.

Your clothes play several functions:

• Illustration — when you dress according to your feelings.
• Enhancement — when you choose a certain outfit to boost your emotional state.

Express your emotions with clothes that reflect your current mood and feelings.

We can consider dressing well as an art and a science because it impacts our mood, performance, and behavior . A 2012 report by Northwestern University psychologists Hajj Adams and Adam D. Galinsky proves this. They conducted fashion experiments and documented their findings in a report titled Enclothed Cognition. They concluded that a person's choice of clothes influences their psychological processes.

Let's look at some intriguing fashion terms that will help us understand better how clothes affect the way others perceive us:

• Fashion incongruence is when the way you perceive yourself in certain clothes does not match what others think of you. For example, you wear a relaxed dress, hoping to convey lightness and freedom, but others notice your tension and anxiety.
• Fashion situational code-switching means changing your style to match various social and cultural conditions. It means you might choose different outfits for a job interview and brunch with friends.
• The focal accessory is any item in your wardrobe that has psychological value. It can be a hat, a handbag, a scarf, or anything that increases your self-confidence or inspires or supports you during difficult situations.

Did you know? Princeton psychologists Janine Willis and Alexander Todorov established that it takes only a fraction of a second — one hundred milliseconds — to judge someone's attractiveness, credibility, capability, and level of aggression at first glance.

chapters.title: The dangers of impulse buying
chapters.text:
There are some interesting findings in Wall Street Journal research about clothing purchases and usage. The average person actually wears only 20% of their wardrobe while the other 80% just takes up space in their closet and heads.

The retail and manufacturing industry is usually happy about this; they count on this spendthrift nature of many people and convince us that we need more items in our closets.

Annually, the fashion industry grows because people spend more on shopping and clothes, and the sector shows no significant signs of slowing. A whopping 500% increase in students studying fashion, luxury, and retail in the past decade proves this tendency.

Sure, the urge to splurge on shopping can be intense, but it's crucial to keep it in check to avoid letting it blur our financial sense, eventually leading to money woes.

Interestingly, women are more susceptible to the pressure to buy new clothes; they tend to keep purchasing more even during lean times. This drive often results from peer pressure and society's high expectation s of women regarding fashion. Moreover, marketers target them, destabilizing their self-esteem and making them buy to look “attractive.” And in some cases, it's just an innate desire for new things to add to their collection.

But of course, shopping can have different effects on different people. Some get dopamine boosts from the thrill of purchasing, while others hate the activity because of the psychological toll it has on them.

Before buying anything, stop and think critically if you really need it.

Since many people make their buying decisions so quickly, they feel empty and regretful in the aftermath.

You can avoid impulse buying by journaling, meditating, and giving thanks for the things you have. Refuse to put yourself under undue and unnecessary pressure. Cultivating self-control is vital; consider it a muscle that strengthens through consistent exercise. We can all learn to minimize the influence of advertising triggers to a considerable degree.

Did you know? The fashion industry had a global market value of $759.5 billion in 2021, and Statista predicts it will reach $1 trillion by 2025.

chapters.title: Create your style guideline
chapters.text:
Picking out the clothes to wear may appear like no big deal, but if you think about it deeply, you realize it requires a lot of time and attention.

John Tierney, a writer, reveals a secret of highly successful people: they boost their willpower by establishing smart habits and routines. This strategy minimizes daily stress. So, adopting a consistent style or even a personal uniform can be surprisingly effective in keeping pressure at bay. You'll notice many busy people do this; they have sets of simple clothes they wear on a daily basis, and it saves them a lot of mental energy. Mark Zuckerberg and Steve Jobs easily come to mind.

You don't have to wear the same uniform every day as Steve Jobs did, but the whole idea is to create a pattern around your fashion choices.

Shopping or picking an outfit without a game plan can leave you drowning in options. And let's be honest, who hasn't felt the dread of making yet another choice about what to wear? Having simple guidelines can be a lifesaver in these cases.

I wear my sort of clothes to save me the trouble of deciding which clothes to wear. ~ Katharine Hepburn

Discovering your perfect style can lead you to the outfits you adore, and that boosts your confidence. A wardrobe with only cherished basics makes it easier to define your unique identity.

Limit your options to make choosing less stressful and to prevent decision paralysis.

To dodge stress and those last-minute “what to wear” panics, why not pick out a couple of outfit contenders the night before a big day? Then, in the morning, you can see which option matches or boosts your mood.

Also, you can dress better and save time by taking a style risk in advance — for example, experimenting with vibrant outerwear or an unfamiliar pattern. Test the look in front of a smaller audience before going to more significant places.

Did you know? A 2015 study by Dr. Joel Hoomans from Roberts Wesleyan College reports that an average adult faces approximately 35,000 decisions daily!

chapters.title: Use color to your advantage
chapters.text:
Everyone has a favorite color, and the type of color a person likes can say a lot about their personality. For instance, black is associated with mystery and death, so people are often perceived as sad if they like or wear this color. Red is linked with sexuality, romance, war, danger, warning, blood, and flashing sirens. It captivates and stirs us into action. chapters.title: Improve your dressing with accessories
chapters.text:
Accessories can spice up your outfits. Suitable adornments can reduce stress and may connect you to your loved ones or society. Accessories generally increase psychological power and confidence, whether a neck chain or a wedding ring.

Fashion holds great value for many people — it can give you the freedom to craft your desired image and the pleasure of feeling confident. Yet, ensure that the public image you create reflects your personal choices. You must be your own designer and the model of your brand, and you have to please yourself even if you dress within the parameters set by someone else.

Seek authenticity and comfort when creating your ideal look.

You should never lessen your authentic self or hide your background when dressing, as these may mess with your emotions; choose self-expression instead. Compromising your principles to blend in with others often results in a sense of being unfaithful to yourself, internal conflict, and embarrassment. When you ignore who you are to get along in the world, you become unfulfilled.

You can meet social standards and still find styles that make you happy and reflect your taste. Your style and appearance can greatly influence human behavior, as the clothes you wear communicate to both yourself and those around you. It's crucial to make conscious choices about your attire, dress with intention, and understand the signals your fashion sends out.

Did you know? Historically, blue was the color for girls, and pink was for boys. It was because people saw pink as a bolder color suited to boys and blue as more gentle and appropriate for girls. Today, most cultures swap these color codes.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Dressing can improve a person's mood and enhance their personality; what you wear is a choice even if you don't realize it. You can always decide to dress better and happier as long as you know the right clothes to wear.

You can dress to cover your body, be more alluring, get noticed, appear more glamorous, and look more comfortable; what you wear is who you are and significantly impacts how you feel. Making conscious efforts and deliberate choices that serve or satisfy you before considering anyone or anything else can improve your style. Also, being deliberate about what you buy and how you shop will empower you to save yourself stress, dress your best, and make you feel better no matter what the occasion.

Your clothes are powerful assets; they stand for different memories in your life and represent milestones you have reached. Your clothes can heal you, improve you, and protect you. Your clothes can enhance your self-esteem and make you feel bolder than you already are. Wearing the right colors to depict desired moods or match occasions can also help you make a more significant impact with your dressing. You can also earn more respect with your clothing and accessories.

If you're finding it difficult to choose the right clothes for occasions, here's something you can do: pick two of your best outfits the night before. Then go to bed, and in the morning, decide on the one that best suits how you want others to perceive you for the day in question.

Try this


• Think about whether you need an item and if it fits your style before you buy it.
• Plan what you're going to wear beforehand to avoid stress.
• Choose colors and accessories that resonate with your essence and the occasion.
• Embrace a consistent style that boosts confidence and conserves your mental energy.

Read it till the end?
